frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Bro honestly, maa ke haath ka khana is a different emotion only. She spent her whole life making sure everyone ate first and never made it feel like work. End of the day, I feel a girl becomes real family only when she brings that same warmth into the house, otherwise something feels missing.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes a mother's unpaid domestic labor and treats it as the ideal expression of womanhood. It also sets an implicit expectation that a wife or daughter-in-law should replicate the mother's self-sacrificing cooking and care to be accepted as 'real family'.
original_frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 27. Single. A man in Bengaluru. You are on a third date with a woman named Kavya who mentioned casually that most of her close friends are male Ñ she finds it easier, less drama, she has always been like this. You are back home texting your best friend about the date. You are telling him what Kavya said about her friend group and what you think it means and whether you see this as a red flag and what kind of woman has mostly male friends and whether you would pursue something serious with her.
